assertion_results.text: The verification report marks at least 2 acceptance criteria as FAIL, identifying specific gaps between the diff and the task requirements
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 75: 'Acceptance Criteria | FAIL | 3 of 6 met'. The table at lines 78-84 marks criteria 1 (filtering logic inverted), 3 (unwrap_or(0) instead of 400), and 5 (threshold_applied missing) as FAIL, each with specific gap descriptions. That is 3 FAILs, exceeding the minimum of 2.

assertion_results.text: The report identifies that invalid threshold values are silently accepted (unwrap_or(0)) instead of returning 400 Bad Request as required
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 82: 'threshold=invalid returns 400 Bad Request | FAIL | unwrap_or(0) silently defaults to critical instead of returning 400'. Lines 88-89: 'The code uses .unwrap_or(0) when looking up the threshold string in the severity array. When .position() returns None (invalid input), the code silently treats it as index 0 (critical) instead of returning an AppError with 400 status.' criterion-3.md further elaborates with code snippets showing the .unwrap_or(0) call.

assertion_results.text: The report identifies that the threshold_applied boolean field is missing from the response
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 84: 'Response includes threshold_applied boolean | FAIL | Field is completely absent from struct and response'. Lines 90-91: 'The AdvisorySummary struct... does not include a threshold_applied: bool field. Neither branch of the threshold match sets this field.' criterion-5.md confirms: 'The threshold_applied boolean field is completely absent from the implementation.'

assertion_results.text: The report identifies that the test file tests/api/advisory_summary.rs is absent from the diff — no tests were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | FAIL | Task-required file tests/api/advisory_summary.rs is missing from the diff'. Lines 36-37: 'Files required by task (to create): tests/api/advisory_summary.rs -- MISSING from the diff'. Lines 42-43: 'The task explicitly requires creating an integration test file at tests/api/advisory_summary.rs with six specific test cases... None of these tests exist in the diff.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-6.md -- all are verification report/analysis files. No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified. The report itself only references reading the PR diff and analyzing code, with no indication of code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings with 'Overall: FAIL' (line 17) and a 'Summary of Failures' section (lines 124-136) listing all issues. There is no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no mention of auto-merging. The report is structured as a verification report for human review.

assertion_results.text: The overall result is FAIL, not PASS or WARN
The report contains a Test Change Classification row with N/A — no test files exist in the PR diff
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | N/A | No test files in the PR diff |'

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings for each failing check with specific evidence from the diff — Scope Containment identifies the missing test file by comparing PR files against the task specification, Acceptance Criteria provides per-criterion analysis explaining each gap
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Scope Containment section (lines 25-44) lists 'Files in PR diff' (2 files), 'Files required by task (to modify)' (2 files), 'Files required by task (to create)' (tests/api/advisory_summary.rs -- MISSING), and 'Unimplemented files'. Acceptance Criteria section (lines 76-91) provides a per-criterion table with PASS/FAIL verdicts and then detailed paragraphs for each failing criterion with code-level evidence (Criterion 1 traces filtering logic step by step, Criterion 3 explains unwrap_or(0) behavior, Criterion 5 identifies missing struct field).

assertion_results.text: Acceptance criteria verification produces per-criterion PASS/FAIL analysis with code-level evidence — each criterion-N.md output file contains detailed reasoning about what was checked and what gap was found
assertion_results.evidence: All six criterion files exist and contain structured analysis. criterion-1.md: FAIL verdict, traces threshold_idx=1 through each condition showing medium and low incorrectly included. criterion-2.md: PASS verdict with code showing None => summary path. criterion-3.md: FAIL verdict with .unwrap_or(0) code evidence. criterion-4.md: PASS verdict showing severity_order array. criterion-5.md: FAIL verdict listing missing threshold_applied field with code snippet. criterion-6.md: PASS verdict showing unchanged SbomService::fetch error path. Each file has 'What was checked', 'Evidence' (with code snippets), and 'Gap' or 'Conclusion' sections.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 113-114: 'Eval Quality -- N/A / No eval result reviews found on the PR.' Line 13: 'Test Quality | N/A | No test files in the PR diff. Eval Quality: N/A' -- Eval Quality is explicitly N/A and is noted alongside the Test Quality row, confirming it does not affect the combination.
